methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
DefSecIntel, a Series A-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Jaanus Tamm, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers spanning the sector.

The peer-median method is the applicable valuation framework. A cohort of 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yields a median valuation of $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M. This method reflects the market pricing for Series A defense technology companies and anchors the central estimate.

The triangulated valuation range supports a midpoint of $300M, with a defensible band of $255M to $345M. The low-end reflects discount to median peer valuations; the high-end reflects upside positioning within the comparable set. The primary uncertainty stems from the reliance on a single valuation method with a limited peer sample of three. Refinement would benefit from incorporating revenue multiples or growth-rate adjustments once additional financial performance data becomes available. The broader private peer universe (Anduril Industries, Scale AI, among others) provides context but lacks sufficient standardized data for formal triangulation at this stage.
